preconditions: Program is decomposed into words.
postconditions: Behavior is represented as composable word definitions.

source_ref.quote_fragment: Everything in Forth is a word.

embedding_text: Forth treats behavior as words; design quality depends on defining small composable words rather than large procedures.

preconditions: A larger behavior can be split into independent steps.
postconditions: Each step becomes a reusable, testable word.

source_ref.quote_fragment: Having a larger set of simpler words makes it easy to use a technique we’ll call factoring.

embedding_text: Factoring means splitting behavior into many small words to improve reuse, testability, and change isolation.

preconditions: Words document consumed and produced stack values.
postconditions: Data moves implicitly between words with minimal call overhead.

source_ref.quote_fragment: Forth uses a stack for passing data.

embedding_text: In Forth, words communicate through the data stack; explicit parameter plumbing is reduced and composition becomes direct.

preconditions: A component boundary is defined.
postconditions: Public words describe component capabilities; internals stay hidden.

source_ref.quote_fragment: We’ll call the set of words which describe a component a lexicon.

embedding_text: A component lexicon is the externally visible set of words that models what the component does while hiding implementation details.

preconditions: A single word mixes many concerns.
postconditions: Low reuse, difficult testing, difficult refactoring.

source_ref.quote_fragment: Forth encourages more words than a conventional program would be into subroutines.

embedding_text: Avoid large monolithic words; they reduce the primary advantage of Forth, which is aggressive factoring into composable units.

preconditions: Internal helper words are used externally.
postconditions: External code depends on internals and breaks under refactor.

source_ref.quote_fragment: internal words support the externally visible lexicon.

embedding_text: Do not expose internal support words as API; only lexicon words should be considered public interface.

preconditions: GETC leaves value c on stack.
TRANSFORM-TO-D consumes c and leaves d.
PUT-D consumes d.
postconditions: Pipeline executes without explicit argument passing variables.

source_ref.quote_fragment: GETC ... TRANSFORM-TO-D ... PUT-D.

embedding_text: Example pipeline: GETC TRANSFORM-TO-D PUT-D demonstrates pure stack-mediated data flow and composition.

preconditions: Each word in a composed phrase has documented stack effect.
postconditions: Net stack effect matches intended interface and leaves no garbage.

source_ref.quote_fragment: provided ... the word doesn’t consume or leave any unexpected values.

embedding_text: Validation rule: composed words must maintain expected net stack effect; unexpected consumption or leftovers indicate a defect.

preconditions: Interactive system can compile and run words immediately.
postconditions: Design-test-refine loops happen in seconds or minutes.

source_ref.quote_fragment: turnaround on the order of seconds.

embedding_text: Forth development is iterative: define small words, test immediately, and refine continuously with fast feedback.

preconditions: Invocation mechanics and data passing are standardized by language model.
postconditions: Code emphasizes domain semantics rather than call boilerplate.

source_ref.quote_fragment: The resulting code becomes a concentrated interplay of words.

embedding_text: Forth removes call and parameter ceremony so code can encode domain meaning as a compact interplay of words.

preconditions: Target word has declared minimum input arity.
postconditions: Execution path rejects or traps stack underflow instead of producing undefined behavior.

source_ref.quote_fragment: word doesn’t consume ... unexpected values.

embedding_text: Test underflow behavior for words that consume stack inputs; missing inputs must be detected and handled predictably.

preconditions: Word is part of a public lexicon or reused across modules.
postconditions: Callers can reason compositionally about required and produced stack values.

source_ref.quote_fragment: words ... require numbers as input ... produce numbers as output.

embedding_text: Require stack-effect comments on public words so agents and humans can compose words safely and retrieve by signature.

preconditions: Word consumes items not declared in its interface or stack-effect comment.
postconditions: Composition fails unpredictably and debugging cost rises sharply.

source_ref.quote_fragment: provided ... doesn’t consume or leave any unexpected values.

embedding_text: Avoid words that rely on undeclared stack items; hidden stack dependencies break composability and reliability.

preconditions: Word body currently mixes multiple concerns.
postconditions: Split words isolate concerns and improve reuse, testability, and replacement.

source_ref.quote_fragment: larger set of simpler words.

embedding_text: Design each word around one semantic action; if a word mixes control, I/O, and transformation, factor it.

preconditions: A component boundary word is declared to leave no residual stack items.
postconditions: Boundary execution leaves exactly the documented net stack effect with no garbage values.

source_ref.quote_fragment: doesn’t consume or leave any unexpected values.

embedding_text: Boundary tests assert that lexicon entry points preserve declared net stack effect and leak no extra values.

preconditions: A new word name is being chosen for public use.
postconditions: Word names encode domain intent, improving retrieval, readability, and model alignment.

source_ref.quote_fragment: model of the component described in simple words.

embedding_text: Name words by domain meaning rather than implementation mechanism so lexicons remain stable under refactor.

preconditions: Words share mutable global state across component boundaries without explicit interface.
postconditions: Change impact becomes non-local and behavior drifts under refactor.

source_ref.quote_fragment: hide information about that module.

embedding_text: Avoid implicit coupling through mutable globals; pass data explicitly or isolate state behind lexicon words.

preconditions: Existing behavior is correct enough to preserve during restructuring.
postconditions: Captured baseline tests prevent accidental semantic regressions while refactoring word boundaries.

source_ref.quote_fragment: incrementally enhancing code that works.

embedding_text: Before refactoring factoring or lexicon boundaries, record characterization tests that lock current behavior.

preconditions: System is in either interpret state or compile state.
postconditions: Words are processed by interpretation or compilation semantics according to state.

source_ref.quote_fragment: in compile state, it performs the compilation semantics of these words.

embedding_text: Forth processing depends on state: interpretation semantics run in interpret state, compilation semantics run in compile state.

preconditions: A word needs non-default compilation behavior.
postconditions: Compilation-time behavior is explicit and interpretation use can be restricted.

source_ref.quote_fragment: use immediate ... compile-only

embedding_text: Use immediate for custom compilation actions and compile-only when interpretive execution should be disallowed.

preconditions: Target word has interpretation semantics that can be represented as an XT.
postconditions: XT can be stored, passed, executed, or compiled for deferred behavior.

source_ref.quote_fragment: ' word gives you the execution token (XT) of a word.

embedding_text: Execution tokens (XTs) are cell-sized handles to interpretation semantics and support higher-order composition through execute and compile,

preconditions: XT-producing and XT-consuming paths are defined.
postconditions: Executing XT preserves declared stack contract and matches direct call semantics.

source_ref.quote_fragment: execute this semantics with execute

embedding_text: For each XT workflow, assert that execute produces the same effect and stack shape as direct invocation.

preconditions: Word definition needs to compile another word's compilation semantics.
postconditions: Generated code reflects compile-time behavior of postponed words.

source_ref.quote_fragment: compile the compilation semantics ... with POSTPONE

embedding_text: POSTPONE compiles a word's compilation semantics, enabling safe construction of immediate/meta words.

preconditions: Potentially failing behavior is encapsulated behind an exception boundary.
postconditions: Failures return exception numbers and restore stack depth at catch boundary.

source_ref.quote_fragment: catch ... catches exceptions and pushes the number of the exception

embedding_text: Use catch/throw to isolate failure boundaries and make exception outcomes explicit as numeric codes.

preconditions: Code mutates state that must be restored on both success and failure.
postconditions: Cleanup executes reliably and exception is rethrown when appropriate.

source_ref.quote_fragment: restore a value upon leaving ... even if ... exception

embedding_text: Wrap state-changing operations with restoration logic so cleanup runs even when throw unwinds control flow.

preconditions: System organizes definitions across multiple vocabularies/wordlists.
postconditions: Name resolution and definition targets are explicitly controlled.

source_ref.quote_fragment: Which wordlists are searched in which order is determined by the search order.

embedding_text: Control namespace behavior with wordlist and search-order operations to avoid accidental name capture.

preconditions: File words return ior status codes.
postconditions: Non-zero ior values are escalated consistently via throw for unified error handling.

source_ref.quote_fragment: All file words return error codes; ... best to pass these error codes with throw

embedding_text: In file workflows, propagate ior through throw immediately to keep I/O error handling uniform and explicit.

preconditions: File descriptors are opened for input/output paths.
postconditions: All opened descriptors are closed on success and failure paths without leaks.

source_ref.quote_fragment: close-file throw

embedding_text: Test that every open-file/create-file call has guaranteed close-file behavior even under read/write exceptions.

preconditions: Interpretive s" results are stored or reused after subsequent s" calls.
postconditions: String pointers become stale or incorrect due to limited lifetime semantics.

source_ref.quote_fragment: string exists only until the next call of s"

embedding_text: Do not persist interpretive s" addresses across later s" usage; the backing storage may be overwritten.

preconditions: Address arithmetic may produce non-cell-aligned addresses.
postconditions: Aligned addresses are used for cell access to preserve correctness and performance.

source_ref.quote_fragment: Aligned ( addr -- a-addr ) produces the next aligned address

embedding_text: Use aligned/align when mixing char-level and cell-level address arithmetic to prevent unaligned cell accesses.

preconditions: Definition pushes data to return stack and fails to pop exactly matched items.
postconditions: Control corruption or crashes due to return-stack imbalance.

source_ref.quote_fragment: If you miscount the return stack items, this usually ends in a crash

embedding_text: Unbalanced >r/r> usage corrupts return-stack state and can crash execution; enforce strict pairing.

preconditions: Definition mixes locals and manual return-stack data storage.
postconditions: Non-portable behavior and potential runtime errors across systems.

source_ref.quote_fragment: You cannot mix using locals and using the return stack

embedding_text: Avoid combining locals and manual return-stack data juggling in the same definition due to portability and safety risks.

preconditions: A family of words shares creation-time data and common runtime behavior.
postconditions: Defining word encapsulates layout and runtime semantics for generated words.

source_ref.quote_fragment: define defining words that create words that do something specific

embedding_text: Use create ... does> to build custom defining words that bind per-instance data with reusable runtime behavior.

preconditions: Program allocates dynamic storage for data structures.
postconditions: Allocation strategy is chosen intentionally between dictionary-allot and heap allocate/free behavior.

source_ref.quote_fragment: allot ... managed like a stack ... alternatively, allocate and free

embedding_text: Differentiate stack-like dictionary allot from heap allocate/free; choose based on required free order and lifetime.
